# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for an S3 bucket to store application assets. Configure the bucket for public read access so our website can serve static files (images, CSS, JavaScript). Keep the template simple for quick deployment.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

Resources:
    BucketNameS3Bucket: # The name of your S3 resource (e.g., 'MyFirstCloudFormationStack')  
        Type           : AWS::S3::Bucket  # Specify the type and location at creation time for bucket resources in Cloudformation as a dict object with two keys, BucketName & Region or LogicalId of an existing S3 resource (e.g., 'MyLogicalResource'). If not specified here then it will be created using logical id "Bucket"
        Properties:  # The properties defined for bucket name and region at creation time are set in a dict object with two keys, BucketName & Region or LogicalId of an existing S3 resource (e.g., 'MyLogicalResource') if not provided here then it will be created using logical id "Bucket".
          BucketName: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}  # Provides a default value when creating stack instances in non-default AWS accounts or regions as specified by the Sub parameter.   If set, must match exactly with an existing S3 bucket name and can't be updated later using this property
          ForcedDestroy: true                     # Specifies whether to destroy all resources even if they are not required (e.g., in a stopped state). Defaults false  when stack instances created within same AWS account or region is specified with force new resource being set as null, and deleted regardless of the value given here
          AccessControlTranslation:  # This property specifies default bucket owner access control for all members to receive 'read' permissions on their own objects. Possible values are "none" (no authentication), "write", or grants lists with an S3 Canonical user ID, group list and entity type ACLs". Default is none
          VersioningConfiguration: # This property specifies default versioning behavior for the bucket if it doesn't have a lifecycle rule. Possible values are 'Enabled', which means Amazon S3 will store all object versions (like retina or zoom levels) within this bucket, and includes an initial request to retrieve these objects along with related metadata about them in every GET response action that is made against the resource using multipart uploads..
            Status: Enabled    # Specifies whether versioning should be enabled for your S3 bucket. Possible values are 'Enabled' or 'Disabled'. Default value if not specified will be set as disabled due to security reason and it could make more data exposure in case of an attack, which can only happen when there is no rules on how the versions would like restored from these buckets
          CorsRule:  # This property defines default cross-origin access control for all object requests. Possible values are a list with one or two elements representing source and method parameters as well as allowed headers parameter in cors configuration . Default will be an empty array if not set which means there is no restrictions on the Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS).
            CorsConfiguration:  # This property defines default cross-origin access control for all object requests. Possible values are a list with one or two elements representing source and method parameters as well as allowed headers parameter in cors configuration . Default will be an empty array if not set which means there is no restrictions on the Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS).
              AllowMethods:  # This property specifies HTTP methods to allow for cross-origin requests from a specified origin. Possible values are 'PUT', and GET'.   If left unspecified, then all CORS request methods include both DELETE & HEAD . Defaults will be ['GET'] if not set
              AllowedOrigins: # This property specifies the origins that should have access to your bucket (cross-origin resource sharing) location. Possible values are a list of valid domain names, or '*' representing all requests from any origin over the network interface IP address with port number and protocol version in CIDR notation format
                - http://example1234567890abcdef0.s3-ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com # a list item example, change this by your real origin URL 
              AllowHeaders:   # This property specifies HTTP headers that are allowed to be included in the server's responses when cross origins requests from specified methods and source as well as Allowed CORS Headers for 'Access-Control-Request-Method', & 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'. Default will include all common request header information.
                - "*"             # a list item example, change this by your real headers   If set to ["*"], then it is disabling any Access Control on the server and allowing requests from anywhere (origin: *). This can impact performance if not done properly as there won't be an actual 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'.
              ExposeHeader: #  Allows a list of response headers to include in cross origin resource sharing policy. Defaults are [] for no restriction, which means it will allow any header inclusion by default (like Allow or Content-Type).   If set as ['*'] then all request method responses includes these allowed CORS Headers if not given special cases and only with common requests headers information is included in the response
            LoggingConfiguration:  # This property specifies logging of bucket level actions. Possible values are 'Enabled' or disabling it (logging off). Defaults to Disabled . If you turn on then logs will be sent using Amazon S3 log event destination ARN and format according BucketLogFormat input parameter
            LifecycleConfiguration:  # This property sets lifecycles for object versions. Possible values are 'Enabled', which means a new version of the existing objects is created before calling transition actions in all but specified exceptions, then transitions occur only on exception calls and not otherwise (excepts if called via multipart upload)
              Rules:  # This property sets lifecycle rules for object versions. Possible values are 'Enabled', which means a new version of the existing objects is created before calling transition actions in all but specified exceptions, then transitions occur only on exception calls and not otherwise (excepts if called via multipart upload)
                - AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload:  # This property specifies whether to abort an incomplete Multipart Upload. Possible values are 'Enabled' or disabling it ('Disabled'). Default is disabled due security reason as the process of completing a MU cannot be stopped, if interrupted can lead into data loss
                  SequenceNumberCount: "1"  # The maximum number that you want to allow in an incomplete multipart upload. Maximum value for allowed values will depend on 'Transition' block property specified inside this bucket lifecycle configuration section (excepts exception calls). Min or required, not both set at the same time
                  Transitions:   # This is a list of transition rules each contains Destination & StorageClass parameters as well and one method call “transition” which sets storage class. If unset then object will be stored in S3 standard when versioning off (Disabled). Defaults to enabled but if not set, must also specify the lifecycle configuration on bucket level
                    - Copy: # This is a copy operation between source & destination parameters as well and another method call “transition” which sets storage class. If unset then object will be stored in S3 standard when versioning off (Disabled). Defaults to enabled but if not set, must also specify the lifecycle configuration on bucket level
                      Destination: # This property specifies where you want your data copied during transition from source ‘S3’. Possible values are 'Migration' or a Amazon S3 URI in CIDR notation format (e..g., s3://mybucket/migrate) for AWS managed account, not both set at the same time
                        StorageClass: Standard # This property defines where your data will reside after it has been copied. Possible values are 'Standard', or a Amazon S3 storage class in CIDR notation format (e..g., s3://mybucket/standard). If unset then default AWS managed backup configuration of the source bucket is used to preserve objects’ lifecycle and versioning settings if any
                      TransitionTimeout: "60"  # This property sets a timeout period for all transition operations in minutes. Possible values are an integer from min = 5 (less than one minute) upto max=1440 mins(more that the default). If unset then defaults to use same settings as S3 standard storage class
                    - Archive: # This is a copy operation between source & destination parameters as well and another method call “transition” which sets transitioning (archive) object's location if needed. Possible values are 'Disabled', or the Amazon Elastic Container Service URL in CIDR notation format(e..g., s3://mybucket/container). If unset then defaults to use same settings as S3 standard storage class
                      StorageClass: Archive # This property defines where your data will reside after it has been copied. Possible values are 'Archive', or a Amazon Elastic Container Service URL in CIDR notation format(e..g., s3://mybucket/archive). If unset then default AWS managed backup configuration of the source bucket is used to preserve objects’ lifecycle and versioning settings if any
                      TransitionTimeout: "120" # This property sets a timeout period for all transition operations in minutes. Possible values are an integer from min = 5 (less than one minute) upto max=1440 mins(more that the default). If unset then defaults to use same settings as S3 standard storage class
